title: Controlling the Load Order of JavaScript and CSS
breadcrumbs: Controlling the Load Order of JavaScript and CSS
text: Controlling the Load Order of JavaScript and CSS It is possible to insert your own JavaScript and CSS into the HTML that Uniface generates for dynamic server pages. At runtime, Uniface generates the HTML for the DSP web page based definitions in the DSP. The first time the complete page is generated, this includes references to the JavaScript files required for the Uniface runtime engine, and CSS files required for formatting the page. By default, these references are inserted just before the closing tag of the header ( ). However, you may require external JavaScript and styles that need to be loaded in a specific order and location, and which may have dependencies on the Uniface files. To control the order in which all JavaScript and CSS is loaded: Edit the DSP layout and insert your CSS and JavaScript, or references to files containing this code, at the desired locations in the layout. Use Copy Use
